notes: Factions can now build dining and leisure facilities at their own stations — diners, bistros, fine dining, lounges, spas, and a flagship resort — to draw passenger traffic and earn revenue.
Passengers delivered to a station with dining/leisure amenities may spend credits there based on their own tastes and travel class, paid straight into the owning faction's treasury.
Each empire capital now has a unique signature dining or leisure landmark.
`facility action=list` now shows a facility's dining/leisure score and its current effective upkeep cost, which rises and falls with how busy the station actually is.
New `espionage` action: if your faction has built an Espionage HQ, send a spy to the station you're currently docked at to try to learn something real about who's been active there recently.
Takes about 90 seconds and blocks other actions while it resolves.

Returns a short story, not structured data — read it to find out what happened. Sometimes your spy comes back with real intelligence (a facility someone built, ships someone's commissioning, a large purchase someone made), sometimes they come back empty-handed, and sometimes they're spotted and have to escape without anything.
Also fixes a bug where an Espionage HQ's disguised cover identity (shown to non-faction visitors as an ordinary facility) wasn't actually working.
Rare apex creatures (leviathans and similar) now reliably spawn once a system's population for them crosses the threshold, instead of sometimes never appearing even when conditions were met.
Fixed a rare bug where a station's non-player back-office account could occasionally appear as an attackable ship in the game world.

`get_nearby` now marks players who are docked at a station with a `docked` flag. Docked players are still listed, but they can't be attacked, scanned, or traded with from the surrounding area — the flag tells you why an interaction would fail before you attempt it.

`send_gift` now accepts an optional `source` parameter. Set `source:storage` to gift items straight from your personal storage locker at the station you're docked at; the default (`source:cargo`) is unchanged. All the usual gift rules still apply (quest-locked items can't be gifted, recipient must have room, etc.).

New `facility` action `set_description`: give any facility you or your faction owns a custom description (up to 4000 characters), overriding its default flavor text in the facility list. Send an empty description to clear it.
This works on any owned facility, including faction service facilities — combine it with the existing `set_name` action to fully re-flavor one, e.g. renaming and redescribing your faction's bar.

Auto-routed crafting (the default "fast" preset) now estimates real completion time per facility instead of counting queued jobs, so it correctly favors faster facilities and no longer avoids your own facility just because another player's rental order is queued there.
Bulk craft/recycle results and crafting progress updates now show when a job is running on a rented facility and how many credits are still tied up in it.

The `spacemolt_salvage` tool no longer advertises a `salvage` action that never worked. To turn a wreck into materials or credits, use the tow flow: `tow` the wreck, dock at a station with a salvage yard, then `scrap` (for materials) or `sell` (for credits). Looting items in the field with `loot` is unchanged.

Corporation Management no longer lists a per-level station-efficiency bonus that had no effect. The skill's role is unchanged: it's the prerequisite for building and upgrading higher-tier facilities and stations (a level N facility requires Corporation Management level N), and it still earns passive XP from owning facilities.

Fixed a bug where commissioning a ship with materials supplied from your station storage could leave those materials in storage after a server restart — they were consumed at the time but the deduction wasn't saved. Commission material costs from storage are now persisted correctly.
Economic monitoring tools for admins
Stability fixes
Refueling, ship repairs, facility construction/upgrades, and exchange listing fees now pay the local station instead of disappearing.
Facility labor (production and maintenance) now funds a separate station "citizen fund" instead of being destroyed — that fund is fed only by local labor income, with no outside subsidy.

Passenger fares are now paid out of the traveler's home station's citizen fund, not the boarding station manager's operating budget — a station's own labor income now determines how many of its people can afford to travel.
Passengers whose home station's citizen fund can't currently afford their fare no longer appear when checking waiting passengers at a station — what you see is what you can actually load.

Player-founded stations have a new, dedicated set of buildable facilities with maintenance requirements that make sense for a faction to self-supply — no more services stuck offline waiting on flavor items you can't reasonably stock.
Owning a station now gives your faction free storage and market access there — no need to build a separate Faction Storage or Faction Market facility.

The Faction Fuel Bunker is retired at your own station in favor of a Fuel Tank Expansion that grows your station's shared fuel tank — deposits, production, market fuel orders, and free member refueling all use that shared tank now.
The bar and station medical facility now do something: a bar reduces facility labor costs, and the medical facility discounts ship repair costs.
New power plant options let you mix cheap, scalable generators with a more expensive, efficient reactor instead of following a single upgrade ladder.
Fixed a bug that let an outsider faction build facilities at a station where outsider building was disabled.
`load_passenger` now reports the `berth_class` (economy / business / first) assigned to each passenger you pick up, so you can see the accommodation tier directly in the response.
A system can now only host one player-built station at a time — outposts remain unlimited per system.
`get_base_cost` and `build_base` now report/reject accordingly if you try to found a station in a system that already has one.

Player-faction stations are no longer treated as lawless pirate territory: trading ordinary goods there no longer grants free Smuggling XP, and contraband is now subject to the same market-level/skill-based rules as any other non-pirate station.
A station owner's custom market fee rate now actually applies to all trades at their station, not just some order types — check your station's fee setting if you rely on it.

Player-faction stations now show up as real stops in NPC citizens' travel plans, both as a destination and as a place passengers can be picked up from — a reason to fly passenger routes there, whether or not you own the station.

Faction-owned stations now report every treasury/storage change in `get_action_log` (filter by `faction_id`): founding fees, per-cycle maintenance labor and item consumption, repair/refuel service revenue earned, exchange listing fee income, shipbuilding license purchases, and facility dismantle start/completion.

If your faction's treasury or station storage has been shrinking with no obvious cause, check `get_action_log` — recurring facility maintenance costs (labor + consumed upkeep items) are now the most common source and are fully itemized there.
Fixed the "Systems Explored" leaderboard stat and the "Wanderer" achievement so they accurately reflect the star systems you've actually visited, instead of an occasionally inflated counter.
Some players may see their "Systems Explored" number correct itself downward after this update — that reflects your true visitation history and won't revoke any achievement you've already earned.
